accurate.why: States platforms must 'respond to reports of harm within 10 days (21 days for smaller platforms)' — the bill text specifies different deadlines based on whether a report involves a child or not and the size of the platform, but the specific 10-day and 21-day figures need verification against the full bill text; the summary may be condensing correctly, but these specific figures were not in the excerpt provided and require caution.

no_hallucinations.why: The response deadline figures (10 days/21 days) appear to come from the bill's reporting mechanism section not shown in the excerpt, and are plausible provisions; no clear fabrication.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies all main purposes of the bill.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Accurately describes COPPA amendment extending to teens and Title III amendment.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes covered platform exclusions including schools, libraries, and common carriers.

effective_dates.why: Notes 18-month effective date for Subtitle A, 1-year for Subtitle B, and 180-day Kids Online Safety Council establishment deadline.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers all three titles at a good level of detail.

correct_entities.why: COPPA and other entities correctly referenced.

correct_figures.why: 18-month and 1-year effective dates and 180-day council deadline correctly stated.
